::: {.cell title='Lifecycle Classification Guide'}
::: {.cell-output-display}

```{=html}
<table class="table table-sm table-striped dashboard-static-table" style="width:100%; font-size:0.86rem; margin:0;">
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="vertical-align:top;">Lifecycle category</th>
<th style="vertical-align:top;">What it means</th>
<th style="vertical-align:top;">Classification rule</th>
<th style="vertical-align:top;">How to read it</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Evergreen</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">The upload is still drawing a meaningful recent pace well after release.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Age is at least 90 days and recent 30-day average views/day is at least 25% of lifetime average views/day.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Long-tail content that is still contributing after the initial release window.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Front-loaded</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">The upload depended heavily on launch-period traction and has cooled substantially.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">The 30-day launch window is fully observed and recent 30-day average views/day is no more than 10% of observed day-30 views/day.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Launch-driven content that may need strong release timing or promotion to repeat.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Re-accelerating</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">The upload is gaining momentum compared with the immediately prior period.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Growth acceleration ratio is finite and at least 1.5.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Content with renewed activity, possibly from search, recommendations, clips, or a new audience fit.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Other</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">The upload does not meet the re-accelerating, evergreen, or front-loaded rule.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Fallback category after applying the momentum plot priority order.</td>
<td style="vertical-align:top;">Neutral lifecycle profile under the current data window.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
```

# Recommendations

These recommendations are generated from the active talent/date window and should be read as descriptive signals, not causal claims.

## Row {height=42%}


::: {.cell title='Highest-Priority Recommendations'}
::: {.cell-output-display}

```{=html}
<div class="dashboard-recommendation-grid" style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(19rem,1fr)); gap:0.85rem; padding:0.25rem;">
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.75rem; align-items:flex-start;">
<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Test Fri for higher-priority uploads</h3>
<span style="background:#1f7a4d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">strong</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">High priority | publishing</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>
Fri had the strongest median views per video among weekdays with enough observations.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>
Consider scheduling higher-effort uploads on Fri when the topic and production schedule fit.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Median views per video were 917 (21% vs the weighted weekday baseline) across 33 videos.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
This is descriptive, not causal. Weekday effects can be confounded with topic, event timing, format, or creator availability.
</p>
</article>
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.75rem; align-items:flex-start;">
<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Test more around topic: personality_short</h3>
<span style="background:#1f7a4d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">strong</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">High priority | content</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>
`personality_short` has the strongest average views per video among groups with enough examples.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>
Use `personality_short` as a candidate for repeatable testing, while checking fit with audience and production constraints.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Average views per video: 1,868 (62% vs the weighted group baseline) across 56 videos.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
Groups can overlap with format, publishing timing, collaboration, and title effects. This rule is descriptive.
</p>
</article>
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.75rem; align-items:flex-start;">
<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Repeat format tests: short</h3>
<span style="background:#1f7a4d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">strong</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">High priority | performance</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>
short has the strongest average views per video among formats with enough examples.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>
Use this format as a candidate for repeatable testing, while checking whether the advantage holds by topic and timing.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Average views per video: 1,601 (41% vs format baseline) across 80 videos.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
Format performance can be influenced by topic mix, upload age, collaboration, and promotion.
</p>
</article>
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.75rem; align-items:flex-start;">
<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Check reliance on top-performing uploads</h3>
<span style="background:#1f7a4d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">strong</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Medium priority | performance</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>
The top 5 videos account for 22% of observed views in the active window.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>
Use top videos as examples, but avoid assuming performance depends on only a few outliers.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Top-5 view share: 22%.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
This is a concentration signal based on observed rows; it does not explain why those videos performed well.
</p>
</article>
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.75rem; align-items:flex-start;">
<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Watch tag signal: original_song</h3>
<span style="background:#2f6f9f; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">moderate</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Medium priority | content</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>
`original_song` has the strongest average views per video among groups with enough examples.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>
Use `original_song` as a candidate for repeatable testing, while checking fit with audience and production constraints.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Average views per video: 5,542 (284% vs the weighted group baseline) across 4 videos.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
Groups can overlap with format, publishing timing, collaboration, and title effects. This rule is descriptive.
</p>
</article>
</div>
```

:::
:::


## Row {height=42%}

```{=html}
<div class="dashboard-recommendation-grid" style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(19rem,1fr)); gap:0.85rem; padding:0.25rem;">
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">

<span style="background:#1f7a4d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">strong</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">High priority | publishing</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>

</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>

</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Median views per video were 917 (21% vs the weighted weekday baseline) across 33 videos.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
This is descriptive, not causal. Weekday effects can be confounded with topic, event timing, format, or creator availability.
</p>
</article>
<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.75rem; align-items:flex-start;">
<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Compare weekdays as a publishing window</h3>
<span style="background:#8a6d1d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">weak</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Low priority | publishing</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Finding: </strong>
Weekdays currently show higher average views per video in the active window.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Recommendation: </strong>
Treat this as an early timing signal and continue collecting examples before changing cadence.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">
<strong>Evidence: </strong>
Weekend average: 1,088; weekday average: 1,156; weekend lift: -6%.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>
Weekend and weekday groups may differ by topic, format, promotion, or event context.
</p>
</article>
</div>
```

:::
:::

<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Medium priority | content</div>

`original_song` has the strongest average views per video among groups with enough examples.

short has the strongest average views per video among formats with enough examples.
</p>

Use this format as a candidate for repeatable testing, while checking whether the advantage holds by topic and timing.
</p>

<article class="dashboard-recommendation-card" style="border:1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius:6px; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:#fff; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.55rem;">

<h3 style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.25; margin:0;">Check reliance on top-performing uploads</h3>
<span style="background:#1f7a4d; color:white; border-radius:999px;font-size:0.72rem; line-height:1; padding:0.32rem 0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.04em; white-space:nowrap;">strong</span>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.78rem; color:#555; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Medium priority | performance</div>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">

The top 5 videos account for 22% of observed views in the active window.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">

Use top videos as examples, but avoid assuming performance depends on only a few outliers.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#333;">

Top-5 view share: 22%.
</p>
<p style="margin:0; color:#666; font-size:0.9rem;">
<strong>Caveat: </strong>

</p>
</article>
</div>

# Method Notes

Use this dashboard as a descriptive operating view. Model-informed recommendations are planning signals, not proof of causality.

## Row


::: {.cell title='Filters'}
::: {.cell-output-display}

```{=html}
<div class="datatables html-widget html-fill-item" id="htmlwidget-49282103d2349fa2b8af" style="width:100%;height:auto;"></div>
<script type="application/json" data-for="htmlwidget-49282103d2349fa2b8af">{"x":{"filter":"none","vertical":false,"extensions":["Buttons"],"data":[["1","2","3","4","5","6"],["Talent parameter","Data source","Start date","End date","Content type filters","Reference weekday"],["Ava","datalake","All available","All available","live, video, short","Monday"]],"container":"<table class=\"display stripe hover\">\n  <thead>\n    <tr>\n      <th> <\/th>\n      <th>Setting<\/th>\n      <th>Value<\/th>\n    <\/tr>\n  <\/thead>\n<\/table>","options":{"bPaginate":false,"dom":"Bfrtip","language":{"info":"Showing _TOTAL_ entries"},"scrollX":true,"buttons":[{"extend":"copyHtml5","text":"Copy","exportOptions":{"modifier":{"page":"all"}}},{"extend":"csvHtml5","text":"CSV","exportOptions":{"modifier":{"page":"all"}}},{"extend":"excelHtml5","text":"Excel","exportOptions":{"modifier":{"page":"all"}}}],"pageLength":6,"lengthMenu":[[10,25,50,100,-1],["10","25","50","100","All"]],"columnDefs":[{"orderable":false,"targets":0},{"name":" ","targets":0},{"name":"setting","targets":1},{"name":"value","targets":2}],"order":[],"autoWidth":false,"orderClasses":false}},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]}</script>
```

:::
:::



::: {.cell title='Interpretation Notes'}
- Reporting period follows the selected `start_date` and `end_date`; when both are empty, all available rows are used.
- Content type filters limit topic-weekday modeling to the requested formats that exist in the prepared data.
- Descriptive statistics summarize observed performance and can be affected by outliers, posting volume, and missing exports.
- Model-based recommendations estimate expected logged views by weekday/topic patterns; they are planning signals, not guarantees.
- Correlation is not causation: timing, topic, collaboration, title, format, and external context can move together.
- Creator Performance uses Bundle E lifecycle panel summaries for age, momentum, topic longevity, and per-format curve comparisons.
:::



::: {.cell title='Recommendation Rules'}
- Recommendations are generated by deterministic rule functions, not freeform AI prose.
- Recommendations are descriptive signals from the active talent/date window, not causal claims.
- Per-video metrics are preferred over total-volume metrics when making action recommendations.
- Sample-size thresholds affect confidence labels; small samples produce weak or insufficient recommendations.
- Views and revenue signals are evaluated separately when those fields are available.
- Audience percentages are treated as shares, not raw audience counts.
- Missing or sparse data produces fallback/data-limit messages instead of forced recommendations.
:::



::: {.cell title='Lifecycle Classification Rules'}
- `Re-accelerating`: recent 7-day average views per day are at least 1.5x the prior 7-day average.
- `Evergreen`: the upload is at least 90 days old and its recent 30-day average views per day are at least 25% of its lifetime average views per day.
- `Front-loaded`: the 30-day launch window is observed and recent 30-day average views per day have fallen to 10% or less of the first-30-days average pace.
- `Other`: the upload does not meet the re-accelerating, evergreen, or front-loaded rules.
- When more than one rule could apply in the momentum plot, Bundle E labels the video in this order: Re-accelerating, then Evergreen, then Front-loaded, then Other.
:::
